sub_questions: What changes (if any) must be made to the canonical overall system architecture specification so it aligns with the runtime realization specification?
How are the two source documents structured today, and what does each currently contain vs. not contain?
Where are the boundaries between the two documents — which topics are runtime-internal (runtime spec authoritative) vs. system-integration (architecture spec authoritative)?
What integration points (interfaces, boundaries, conditions, rules) must the canonical architecture spec make explicit so companion subsystem documents plug in cleanly?
What runtime-driven concepts must be propagated UP into the architecture spec at the integration-surface level WITHOUT duplicating runtime internal details?
Where does the canonical architecture spec currently duplicate runtime internals, and what should be removed or replaced with a pointer to the runtime spec?
Are there design contradictions between the two documents that cannot be resolved by the 'runtime realization spec is authoritative on runtime concerns' heuristic?
What division-of-responsibility guidance applies to companion subsystem documents (i.e., other subsystem specs that will sit alongside the runtime spec under the canonical architecture umbrella)?

When the specs reference vendor / borrowed technology (Effect, Inngest, oRPC, OCLIF, Elysia, Bun, harness/native-host), do their integration claims hold up against the vendor's actual capability/lifecycle/integration shape — and only where load-bearing for an alignment recommendation?

entities.name: RAWR Effect Runtime Realization System Canonical Spec
required_fields: per-section duplication-vs-complementarity judgement vs. sibling spec

entities.name: RAWR Canonical Architecture Spec
required_fields: per-section duplication-vs-complementarity judgement vs. sibling spec

entities.name: companion subsystem documents
required_fields: integration contract the architecture spec must expose

entities.name: Effect
required_fields: Layer composition / provider provisioning if load-bearing

entities.name: oRPC
required_fields: procedure middleware / caller-class boundary if load-bearing

entities.name: OCLIF
required_fields: command lifecycle / plugin model if load-bearing

entities.name: prior alignment final report
entities.use: comparison-only — read once after fresh report is final, may surface a brief 'Comparison to prior run' appendix; never cite, never use as draft, never consume as evidence

required_formats: two gospel-rank source-analysis notes (one per spec) with full ToC + line numbers, scope/exclusions/authority claims, integration boundaries, per-section duplication-vs-complementarity judgements
vendor-verification notes tagged distinctly from spec-analysis notes (e.g., kind: vendor-verification) with explicit URL + library version citations
in-report citations using note IDs plus line refs back to source spec lines for spec evidence, and URL + library version for vendor evidence
per-recommendation traceability (each recommended change must point at the spec section/line it modifies and the runtime-spec evidence that drives it)
explicit contradiction flags (terminal section 5) where the runtime-authoritative heuristic does not resolve a conflict
required_sections: ## 2. Integration surface the canonical architecture spec must own
## 3. Runtime-driven additions the canonical architecture spec is missing
## 4. Recommended changes to the canonical architecture specification
## 6. Division-of-responsibility guidance for companion subsystem documents
required_section_headings: ## 2. Integration surface the canonical architecture spec must own
## 3. Runtime-driven additions the canonical architecture spec is missing
## 4. Recommended changes to the canonical architecture specification
## 6. Division-of-responsibility guidance for companion subsystem documents
scope_conditions: For all runtime concerns, the runtime realization spec is authoritative; the canonical architecture spec must NOT duplicate runtime internals.
The canonical architecture spec's role is the core integration document explaining how the entire platform works at a system level.
Width corpus is primarily the two source spec files; vendor verification only when a claim is load-bearing for an alignment recommendation.
Vendor research uses mcp__docs__resolve-library-id + mcp__docs__query-docs FIRST; falls back to WebFetch / WebSearch / GitHub for primary-source confirmation.
Skip generic web breadth, adversarial web search, and academic search — there are no relevant external sources for the alignment question itself.
Source spec files live in the main worktree path (rawr-hq-template/docs/projects/rawr-final-architecture-migration/resources/spec/), NOT in this worktree — that is the canonical location.

The prior final report at research/_comparison/prior_final_report_runtime-canon-arch-align.md is COMPARISON-ONLY; never cite, never use as draft, never consume as evidence; read once after fresh report is complete to surface a brief 'Comparison to prior run' appendix.
When contradictions cannot be resolved by the 'runtime realization spec is authoritative on runtime concerns' heuristic, flag explicitly in terminal section 5 — do not silently resolve.
